concept: Habitat / Place and Conditions Supporting an Organism
description: A place together with the conditions that support a stated organism or living group. The same grove can be habitat in one season and not the next, and an animal seen at a place may only be passing through; whether the place feeds and shelters it is a further question.
sound_symbolism: Soft 'me' closes gently at the lips and moves into stressed dental 'nu'. Together 'me.nu' preserve the first two syllables of 'menui' (dwell), a familiar sheltered resonance. 'Ro' rolls around that center and rounds the ending into a place where the mouth can enter, settle, and turn.
grammatical_notes: A countable or mass noun commonly modified by the organism or group: 'ha koshira ha teluno menuro nai.' (This grove is a habitat for this amphibian.) Name life stage, season, conditions, and scale where they alter the claim. Distinct from 'lokue' (place), 'womu' (home), 'menui' (dwell), and an ecosystem as a wider analytical whole.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Habitat vocabulary makes the material conditions of other beings visible in decisions about land, infrastructure, work, and restoration.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Situated knowledge attends to where beings feed, shelter, reproduce, rest, and move rather than treating land as empty background.

semantic_domains.nature: the place and conditions in which a living group dwells
semantic_domains.spatial: a bounded or distributed area understood relative to an organism
